{{Infobox book
| name = City of Light
| slug = tg-03-city-of-light
| precedes = [[Of Sea and Shadow (novel)|Of Sea and Shadow]]
| follows = [[The Crimson Vault (novel)|The Crimson Vault]]
| cover = [[File:Cover-tg-03-city-of-light.jpg|250px]]
| released = 2014
| isbn = 9780989671712
| publisher = Hidden Gnome Publishing
| pages = 392
}}

== Plot summary ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Following a devastating battlefield conflict, the Eldest{{!}}Eldest Nye retrieves a blood-red [[Ragnarus sword]] and the body of his former master, the The Wanderer{{!}}Wanderer, preparing to return them to [[Valinhall]]. In the wake of this, King Zakareth VI{{!}}King Zakareth becomes empowered by the [[Ragnarus Incarnation]] and conspires with [[Queen]] [[Cynara]] to target [[Elysia]]. Simultaneously, [[Alin]] arrives in [[Enosh]] as the new [[Elysian Incarnation]], initiating a violent purge of the [[Grandmasters]] to reshape the city into an unnatural paradise. This upheaval forces [[Simon]], who is training in [[Valinhall]], into a series of trials involving the resurrected ghost of [[Valin]]—the man [[Simon]] previously killed. Through these tests, [[Simon]] learns that [[Valin]] has been brought back as a bound guardian, and he eventually receives luminous green armor to survive lethal magical attacks.

As [[Alin]]'s regime in [[Enosh]] grows increasingly oppressive, Queen Leah I{{!}}Queen Leah organizes a mission to gather intelligence, dispatching [[Simon]] and [[Indirial]] into the city. The tension escalates when [[Alin]] confronts [[Leah]] and [[Simon]] at a waystation, demanding custody of [[Ilana]]. A high-stakes magical duel ensues, during which [[Simon]] uses his The mask{{!}}mask to deliver devastating blows to [[Alin]]. Amidst this chaos, [[Valinhall]] is attacked by a mechanical giant named [[Tartarus]], and [[Leah]] is forcibly transported to the territory of [[Avernus]]. In [[Avernus]], [[Leah]] learns that her father is an [[Incarnation]] and discovers a historical pattern of [[Travelers]] being manipulated by the [[Damascan]] crown to maintain control over the Territories.

The conflict reaches a breaking point when King Zakareth VI{{!}}King Zakareth, seeking to rule all territories, captures [[Indirial]] and holds his family hostage. To survive, [[Indirial]] is forced to embrace his identity as the [[Valinhall Incarnation]]. This leads to a massive invasion of [[Valinhall]] by [[Zakareth]] and his coordinated forces. During the assault, [[Kai]] engages in a desperate duel with [[Indirial]] at the walls of [[Cana]]; however, the battle turns tragic when [[Kai]] undergoes a grotesque transformation and dies. [[Simon]] intervenes in the metaphysical crisis, ultimately defeating [[Indirial]] but collapsing from the effort. Meanwhile, [[Leah]] enters the [[Crimson Vault]] to bargain with the spirit of [[Queen]] [[Cynara]], who demands the [[Eye of Ages]] in exchange for wisdom.

In the final stages of the conflict, [[Simon]] accepts his destiny as the [[Founder's heir]] and claims the [[sword]] [[Mithra]]. [[Leah]] returns to [[Valinhall]] with vital intelligence, revealing [[Zakareth]]'s plan to assault [[Enosh]]. As [[Zakareth]] and five other [[Incarnations]] launch a massive invasion, [[Simon]] and the [[Valinhall Travelers]] face an onslaught of enemies. To prevent [[Simon]] from succumbing to the lethal debt of his powers, [[Alin]] uses the [[White Light]] to empower him, allowing [[Simon]] to defeat the remaining [[Incarnations]]. This surge of power enables [[Leah]] to use a [[Hanging Tree]] to successfully seal away King Zakareth VI{{!}}King Zakareth. In the aftermath, stability is restored: [[Simon]] accepts his roles as the leader of [[Valinhall]] and [[the Queen]]'s protector, [[Alin]] opens [[Elysia]] to students, and the Eldest{{!}}Eldest Nye sets [[Simon]] on a new quest to find the last soldier of the [[Dragon Army]].}}

== Chapter-by-chapter ==
* '''Chapter 1:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Nye Eldest{{!}}The Eldest Nye moves stealthily through a devastated battlefield, observing the aftermath of a conflict involving the [[Valinhall Incarnation]] and various human and inhuman casualties. After locating the corpse of his former master, the The Wanderer{{!}}Wanderer, the [[Eldest]] retrieves a blood-red [[Ragnarus sword]] and prepares to transport the body back to [[Valinhall]]. He concludes his task by acknowledging a second, golden-lined blade left behind on the field.}}
* '''Chapter 2:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|King Zakareth VI{{!}}King Zakareth, now empowered by the [[Ragnarus Incarnation]], conspires with [[Queen]] [[Cynara]] to secure his rule and target [[Elysia]] as a primary threat. Meanwhile, [[Alin]] arrives in [[Enosh]] as an [[Incarnation]] himself, using his new powers to confront and execute [[Grandmaster Helgard]] after accusing the leadership of endangering the world. As [[Alin]] begins a purge of the [[Grandmasters]] to reshape the city, [[Ilana]] sets out to find her brother, while [[Simon]] prepares for the possibility of having to kill him.}}
* '''Chapter 3:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|While waiting in a snowy mountain valley, [[Simon]] is attacked by a six-armed creature and subsequently confronted by the [[Helgard Incarnation]], a woman who seeks to capture him. [[Simon]] engages in a chaotic battle alongside [[Indirial]] and Queen Leah I{{!}}Queen Leah's forces, managing to wound Incarnation{{!}}the Incarnation before she escapes through a mysterious red-lined Gate. The chapter concludes with [[Leah]] identifying the portal as the entrance to the [[Crimson Vault]] rather than [[Helgard]].}}
* '''Chapter 4:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah]] struggles with the unsettling realization that a [[Helgard Incarnation]] has entered Ragnarus the Crimson Vault{{!}}Ragnarus, leading to a tense confrontation with Feiora Torannus{{!}}Overlord Feiora and the arrival of [[Grandmaster Naraka]], who warns that [[Enosh]] is being invaded by the [[Elysian Incarnation]]. Meanwhile, [[Simon]] continues his training within [[Valinhall]], where an encounter with the Eldest{{!}}Eldest Nye leads him to a mysterious graveyard. Upon entering the graveyard, [[Simon]] is shocked to encounter the apparent ghost of [[Valin]], the man he previously killed.}}
* '''Chapter 5:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah]] interrogates [[Grandmaster Naraka]], who reveals that [[Alin]] has become the [[Elysian Incarnation]] and is transforming the city of [[Enosh]] into a golden, unnatural paradise. Meanwhile, [[Simon]] witnesses a duel between [[Kai]] and [[Valin]], discovering that the [[Eldest]] has resurrected [[Valin]] as a bound guardian of [[Valinhall]] to serve a mysterious task. The chapter concludes with [[Valin]] challenging [[Simon]] to a fight within the graveyard.}}
* '''Chapter 6:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|After a training session with [[Valin]], [[Simon]] learns from [[Kai]] that the master is trapped within [[Valinhall]] due to a debilitating wound. [[Valin]] then initiates a true test for the ghost armor, transforming into an [[Incarnation]] and forcing [[Simon]] to dodge lethal light arrows from a magical bow. Just as [[Simon]] is pinned down and facing certain death, a suit of luminous green armor appears to save him.}}
* '''Chapter 7:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Queen Leah I{{!}}Queen Leah and [[Overlord Indirial]] debate a high-risk scouting mission to the reconstructed city of [[Enosh]], which is being reshaped by Incarnation{{!}}the Incarnation [[Alin]]. After [[Indirial]] reveals he has already sent several teams to scout the area, [[Leah]] agrees to join a mission led by [[Indirial]] and Feiora Torannus{{!}}Overlord Feiora. Meanwhile, [[Alin]] attempts to use his powers to heal a dying worker in [[Enosh]], but his failure and subsequent use of the [[Blue Light]] to end the man's life leave him struggling with conflicting internal voices.}}
* '''Chapter 8:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|After [[Valin]] reveals the true nature of the graveyard test, [[Simon]] searches for his missing The mask{{!}}mask while dealing with a loud communication crystal and an indignant [[Otoku]]. [[Indirial]] arrives to collect [[Simon]], leading to a tense confrontation between him, [[Valin]], and [[Kai]] before [[Simon]] is escorted to the [[workshop]] by [[Kai]]. In the gallery, [[Olissa]] explains the mechanics of The mask{{!}}mask-making and [[Valinhall]]'s power, only for [[Simon]] to discover that [[Kai]] had been secretly carrying his The mask{{!}}mask the entire time.}}
* '''Chapter 9:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|After [[Indirial]] and [[Simon]] return from [[Valinhall]] with the news that Incarnation{{!}}the Incarnation [[Valin]] has returned, [[Leah]] organizes a team to infiltrate the city of [[Enosh]]. They interrogate a captured guard who reveals that [[Alin]] has taken control of the city, executed several [[Grandmasters]], and established a strict, fearful regime. [[Leah]] then dispatches [[Simon]] and [[Indirial]] into the city to gather intelligence while she remains behind to coordinate their support.}}
* '''Chapter 10:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|While conducting an inspection of the [[Violet District]], [[Alin]] listens to [[Rhalia]] recount how she once became a monster by [[binding]] [[Incarnations]] to her service. Meanwhile, [[Simon]] observes the oppressive and chaotic state of [[Enosh]] under [[Alin]]'s rule before reuniting with [[Ilana]] at a waystation. As [[Leah]], [[Simon]], and others prepare to depart for [[Cana]], [[Alin]] suddenly enters the waystation, causing a moment of intense tension.}}
* '''Chapter 11:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Alin]], the [[Elysian Incarnation]], confronts [[Leah]] and [[Simon]] at a waystation, demanding custody of [[Grandmaster Naraka]] and his sister [[Ilana]]. After [[Alin]] uses his power to restrain [[Ilana]], [[Simon]] dons a The mask{{!}}mask to activate his abilities and engages [[Alin]] in a high-stakes magical duel. Meanwhile, King Zakareth VI{{!}}King Zakareth of Ragnarus the Crimson Vault{{!}}Ragnarus learns of the conflict and releases several [[Incarnations]] to further his own agenda.}}
* '''Chapter 12:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|As the battle rages in the streets, [[Indirial]] prevents [[Leah]] from joining the fight against [[Alin]] while [[Grandmaster Naraka]] is restrained and the [[Naraka Gate]] is sealed. [[Leah]] manages to strike [[Alin]] with her [[Lightning Spear]], though he survives through multiple layers of magical shields. Meanwhile, a Valinhall Travelers{{!}}Valinhall Traveler engages [[Alin]] in combat before [[Simon]] descends from the sky to deliver a devastating series of blows that leave [[Alin]] severely wounded.}}
* '''Chapter 13:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|In [[Valinhall]], [[Lycus]] and [[Andra]] are interrupted during a sparring session by the arrival of an unstable Gate that releases a faceless, mechanical giant named [[Tartarus]]. As [[Andra]] engages the intruder, [[Lycus]] flees to find help. Meanwhile, outside [[the House]], [[Simon]] engages in a violent battle with the resurrected [[Incarnation]] of [[Alin]], eventually surviving the encounter only to collapse from exhaustion after removing his The mask{{!}}mask.}}
* '''Chapter 14:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah]] is forcibly transported to the territory of [[Avernus]] by Feiora Torannus{{!}}Feiora after [[Alin]], the [[Incarnation of Elysia]], resists her magical [[binding]] and seizes control of the battlefield. Meanwhile, in [[Valinhall]], a massive Tartarus Incarnation{{!}}Incarnation of Tartarus invades [[the House]], causing destruction and threatening [[Lycus]]'s father. The swordsman [[Kai]] eventually intervenes, using his wind-enhanced powers to destroy the mechanical giant, leaving the Eldest{{!}}Eldest Nye to investigate the mysterious nature of the attack.}}
* '''Chapter 15:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Zakareth]], accompanied by the [[Incarnations]] of [[Helgard]] and [[Lirial]], uses a Gate to travel through the [[Crystal Fields]] toward [[Leah's camp]]. Meanwhile, [[Alin]] experiences a moment of profound clarity under the influence of [[Violet Light]], realizing he has become a villainous figure before his powers fade. As [[Alin]] retreats from the exhausted Valinhall Travelers{{!}}Valinhall Traveler, Feiora Torannus{{!}}Overlord Feiora escapes through a Gate with [[Simon]], while [[Ilana]] is transported to the vibrant [[Territory]] of [[Elysia]] by a [[Traveler]] named [[Rhalia]].}}
* '''Chapter 16:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|After waking from paralysis, [[Simon]] learns from the Eldest{{!}}Eldest Nye that he is being positioned as the heir to [[Valin]], the [[Founder]] of [[Valinhall]]. [[Simon]] seeks guidance from the resurrected The Wanderer{{!}}Wanderer, [[Valin]], who warns him that the [[Eldest]] intends to manipulate him into serving the [[Territory]]'s expansion at any cost. [[Valin]] encourages [[Simon]] to embrace his potential as a leader rather than being a puppet, leading [[Simon]] to resolve to reclaim his own agency from the [[Eldest]].}}
* '''Chapter 17:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|After [[Simon]] opens a Gate to [[Cana]] for Feiora Torannus{{!}}Feiora, [[Indirial]] returns to his camp only to find it under attack by the [[Helgard]] and [[Lirial]] [[Incarnations]]. The [[Incarnations]] capture [[Indirial]] and transport him to the [[Crimson Vault]], where he discovers that King Zakareth VI{{!}}King Zakareth has Incarnated and is holding [[Indirial]]'s wife and daughter hostage. [[Zakareth]] reveals his goal is to force [[Indirial]] to [[Incarnate]], leaving the Overlord to struggle with how to survive without losing his humanity.}}
* '''Chapter 18:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah]], physically exhausted and temporarily stripped of her Ragnarus the Crimson Vault{{!}}Ragnarus powers, arrives at a [[Corvinus tribe]] outpost in [[Avernus]] and is led to meet the Avernus Incarnation{{!}}Incarnation of Avernus. During their conversation, Incarnation{{!}}the Incarnation reveals that [[Leah's father]] is an [[Incarnation]] and explains a historical pattern where [[Travelers]] were manipulated into transforming outside of their Territories. [[Leah]] realizes that the long period of peace without wild [[Incarnations]] likely benefited the [[Damascan]] crown by making the Territories easier to control and exploit.}}
* '''Chapter 19:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|While being tortured by various [[Incarnations]] under King Zakareth VI{{!}}King Zakareth's watch, [[Indirial]] resists the urge to call upon his [[Valinhall powers]] until he realizes a rescue attempt by [[Simon]] is merely an illusion created by the [[Asphodel Incarnation]]. [[Indirial]] decides to embrace his identity as [[Valinhall]] to fight back, while elsewhere, [[Leah]] discusses the unnatural nature of Incarnation{{!}}the Incarnation system with the [[Avernus Incarnation]]. The [[Avernus Incarnation]] advises [[Leah]] on restoring balance to the world and gifts her a mind-reading raven before sending her home.}}
* '''Chapter 20:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] engages in a high-stakes battle with King Zakareth VI{{!}}King Zakareth, utilizing his newfound connection to the [[Valinhall]] diamond to resist the King's commands and eventually rescue his family from their cages. After a massive explosion of red light from a [[Ragnarus Gate]], [[Indirial]] is buried under rubble but is ultimately rescued and allied with [[Zakareth]]. Meanwhile, the [[Eldest]] takes custody of [[Indirial]]'s female captives, intending to use them as leverage to influence Incarnation{{!}}the Incarnation.}}
* '''Chapter 21:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Following the disappearance of [[Indirial]], an [[Endross Incarnation]] launches a deceptive attack on [[Leah's camp]] that causes chaos without inflicting actual casualties. Meanwhile, King Zakareth VI{{!}}King Zakareth and a transformed [[Indirial]] coordinate an invasion of [[Valinhall]] to seize the Dragon's Fang{{!}}Dragon's Fangs and disarm its [[Travelers]]. As [[Indirial]]'s forces battle [[the Nye]] and the [[Tartarus Incarnation]], [[Kai]] discovers that his beloved dolls have been brutally damaged during the assault on [[the House]].}}
* '''Chapter 22:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|In the [[Damascan camp]], King Zakareth VI{{!}}King Zakareth (the [[Ragnarus Incarnation]]) stages a fake battle to force the [[Endross Incarnation]] into submission and declares his intent to rule all territories. Meanwhile, [[Kai]] engages in a desperate, high-stakes duel against [[Indirial]] (the [[Valinhall Incarnation]]) at the walls of [[Cana]], fueled by rage over the destruction of his home. As [[Leah]] enters the [[Crimson Vault]] to investigate her father's motives, [[Simon]] experiences a sudden surge of grief from his weapon, Azura, revealing a vision of the murdered [[Otoku]] and prompting him to prepare for battle.}}
* '''Chapter 23:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] engages in a high-stakes duel with [[Kai]] on the walls of [[Cana]], relishing the thrill of combat despite being wounded by [[Kai]]'s weapons. The battle shifts dramatically when [[Kai]] completes his transformation, causing a metaphysical crisis between the two [[Incarnations]] and allowing [[Simon]] to intervene with a powerful The mask{{!}}mask. Ultimately, [[Simon]]'s overwhelming strength defeats [[Indirial]], leaving the latter trapped inside his [[Territory]] while [[Simon]] collapses from the effort.}}
* '''Chapter 24:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Following a violent battle, [[Simon]] attempts to save [[Kai]] from a grotesque, incomplete [[Incarnation transformation]], but is ultimately prevented by [[Andra]] and [[Erastes]] from intervening after [[Kai]] dies surrounded by his dolls. Meanwhile, [[Leah]] enters the [[Crimson Vault]] to bargain with the spirit of [[Queen]] [[Cynara]], seeking information on how to stop her father. The chapter concludes as [[Cynara]] demands the [[Eye of Ages]] in exchange for wisdom, beginning a ritual to take [[Leah]]'s eye.}}
* '''Chapter 25:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|After being provoked by [[Valin]] and the Eldest{{!}}Eldest Nye, [[Simon]] accepts the responsibility of becoming the [[Founder's heir]] and claims the [[sword]] [[Mithra]]. [[Leah]] uses a [[gatecrawler]] to reach [[Valinhall]], returning lost Dragon's Fang{{!}}Dragon's Fangs to [[Simon]] and revealing that King Zakareth VI{{!}}King Zakareth is preparing a massive assault on [[Enosh]]. To prepare for the coming battle, [[Leah]] orchestrates a plan to use a discovered [[Seed of the Hanging Tree]] and leads her group through a Gate into [[Naraka]] to track a prisoner.}}
* '''Chapter 26:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Following a period of meditation to regain his composure, [[Alin]] faces an imminent invasion by King Zakareth VI{{!}}King Zakareth and five other [[Incarnations]]. During the chaos, [[Grandmaster Naraka]] attempts to attack [[Alin]] but is intercepted and brutally neutralized by [[Simon]] and his [[Valinhall]] allies. The chapter concludes with [[Alin]], [[Leah]], and [[Simon]] developing a strategic plan to divide and defeat the enemy [[Incarnations]], bolstered by the timely arrival of [[Denner]] and [[Kathrin]].}}
* '''Chapter 27:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|As the battle for [[Enosh]] intensifies, [[Simon]] and the [[Valinhall Travelers]] face an onslaught from several [[Incarnations]] who use a [[gatecrawler]] to breach [[Elysia]]. To save [[Simon]] from the lethal debt of his [[Valinhall powers]], [[Alin]] uses the [[White Light]] to empower him with immense strength. This surge of power allows [[Simon]] to defeat the remaining [[Incarnations]], ultimately helping [[Leah]] use a [[Hanging Tree]] to seal away King Zakareth VI{{!}}King Zakareth.}}
* '''Chapter 28:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Queen Leah I{{!}}Queen Leah grants Feiora Torannus{{!}}Overlord Feiora guardianship of her brother and introduces [[Gilad]], a young man who helped secure the surrender at [[Enosh]]. During an official awards ceremony, [[Simon]] accepts his roles as the leader of [[Valinhall]] and [[the Queen]]'s personal protector, while [[Alin]] announces that [[Elysia]] is now accepting students. Following the ceremony, the [[Eldest]] instructs [[Simon]] to seek out the last soldier of the [[Dragon Army]] to ensure she fulfills her duties to [[Valinhall]].}}

== Characters ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Simon]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Alin]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Kai]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Eldest]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Valin]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Zakareth]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Andra]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[King Zakareth VI]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Grandmaster Naraka]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Erastes]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Otoku]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Caela]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Valinhall Incarnation]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Rhalia]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Rebekkah]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Lirial Incarnation]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Korr]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Elysian Incarnation]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Feiora Torannus]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Avernus Incarnation]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Eugan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Ornheim Incarnation]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Denner]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Gloria]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Helgard Incarnation]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Karoshan queen (The Traveler's Gate Trilogy)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Eliadel]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Kathrin]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Tartarus Incarnation]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Murin]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Benson]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Lycus]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Elysian Traveler]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Elaina]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Cynara]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Queen Leah I]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Incarnation of Elysia]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Lilia]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Andra Agnos]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Olissa]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[gatecrawler]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Overlord of Cana]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Angeline]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[The Wanderer]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Makko]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[King of Damasca]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Caius]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Lysander]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Shai]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Ilana]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Grandmaster Helgard]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[King Zakareth the Sixth]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Helgard Travelers]]}}

== Locations & groups ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Valinhall]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Elysia]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Enosh]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Cana]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Crimson Vault]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Valinhall Travelers]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Helgard]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Avernus]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Naraka]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Tartarus]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Endross]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Damasca]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Travelers]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Nye]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Avernus Travelers]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[House]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Dragon Army]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[City of Light]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[House of Blades]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|graveyard}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Ornheim]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Blue District]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Naraka waystation]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Violet District]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Vault (The Traveler's Gate Trilogy)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Myria]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Bel Calem]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Ornheim Travelers]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Red District]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Corvinus tribe]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Unnamed World]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Overlords (The Traveler's Gate Trilogy)]]}}

== Abilities & sacred arts ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Ragnarus the Crimson Vault]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Lirial]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Hanging Tree]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Lightning Spear]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Orange Light]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Blue Light]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Rose Light]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Alin's Territory]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Red]]}}

== Artifacts & constructs ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Dragon's Fang]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Mithra]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Vasha]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Azura (The Traveler's Gate Trilogy)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|ghost armor}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[The mask]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Benson's steel]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Lirial crystal]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Ragnarus Gate]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Rod of Harmony]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Seijan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Ragnarus weapon]]}}

== Events & concepts ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Incarnation]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Silver Light]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Green Light]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Nye essence]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[gold light (The Traveler's Gate Trilogy)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Endross Incarnation]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Violet Light]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Incarnations]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Valinhall Gate]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Ragnarus Incarnation]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Asphodel]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Territory]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Naraka Gate]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[gate (The Traveler's Gate Trilogy)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Naraka Traveler]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Naraka Incarnation]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Asphodel Incarnation]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Ragnarus Traveler]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Valinhall powers]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Red Light]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[White Light]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Traveler]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Tartarus steel]]}}

== Sources ==
* https://openlibrary.org/works/OL27390340W

{{Willverse books nav}}

[[Category:Books]]
[[Category:City of Light]]
